programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark tries to clear a surfer suspected of murdering his girlfriend's brother, a member of a loutish street gang. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates the case of his old friend Kent Morrissey, who mysteriously dropped dead while carrying out an operation. Guest starring Jack Klugman

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The co-pilot of a plane carrying Dr Sloan to a conference is murdered by persons unknown mid-flight, and the remainder of the crew members find themselves struck down by a mystery illness. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Paige must simplify the science for the geniuses of Team Scorpion when their mental faculties are impaired during a job to prevent the creation of a black hole

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team inadvertently crashes a bank robbery in progress and soon find themselves taken hostage. Meanwhile, Sylvester's feelings for Florence grow

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An internal security breach forces NCIS to go into lockdown when a covert listening device is found in a pen belonging to Secretary of the Navy Sarah Porter. DiNozzo discovers that National Security Agency analyst Ellie Bishop wrote a report two years previously predicting the possibility of such an occurrence and she is brought in to assist with the investigation and track down the culprit. Guest starring Leslie Hope and Emily Wickersham

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when marine corporal Flegman is found shot to death, and a phone leads them to a hotel room where Gibbs awkwardly stumbles upon his ex-wife Diane Sterling and her FBI agent ex-husband TC Fornell. She had been attempting to call her boyfriend Eddie to break up with him, but somehow Flegman had taken possession of his phone. The team tracks down Eddie's car and discovers a clown nose along with traces of blood and he quickly becomes the prime suspect

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when a mysterious, fast-spreading illness strikes children from military families. While Gibbs tries to determine the cause, Abby and Jimmy partner with the Naval Medical Research Centre in the hope of delivering a cure before the holidays. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed by a stolen surveillance drone and the team links it to elusive terrorist Benham Parsa. NCIS joins forces with the Department of Defence to track down the device before it is used for a large-scale attack, but Gibbs is less than pleased that he has to work with his ex-lover Hollis Mann. Meanwhile, McGee hopes he will be granted time off to attend a black-tie gala honouring his girlfriend Delilah. Guest starring Susanna Thompson (Arrow)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In an effort to find the terrorist Parsa, Gibbs and the NCIS team track down one of his cohorts using evidence gleaned from the drone attack which has killed six people and wounded a further 22. Meanwhile, an emotionally shaken McGee struggles with the ramifications of the incident. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, Sean Murray, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Paige must simplify the science for the geniuses of Team Scorpion when their mental faculties are impaired during a job to prevent the creation of a black hole

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team inadvertently crashes a bank robbery in progress and soon find themselves taken hostage. Meanwhile, Sylvester's feelings for Florence grow

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homeland Security employee Victor Sterling mysteriously disappears and his car is found at a drive-through restaurant that becomes the scene of a double murder. To complicate matters further, the missing man's outspoken spouse happens to be the ex-wife of both Gibbs and Fornell. American naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, Joe Spano, Tom Gallop and Melinda McGraw

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A military aircraft transporting the bodies of dead US marines back home crashes before reaching its destination, and Gibbs and the team are called in to help identify human remains at the site of the wreckage. However, a DNA discrepancy turns a routine investigation into a search for a missing person. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ziva and Gibbs lead the mission to rescue the captured Marine in Afghanistan before discovering that her captor's brothers have bigger plans and are at large in America. Gibbs has to be stopped when he tries to employ methods similar to those used on Kinah and Lala in an effort to gain information, but Vance has a trick that gives the agents an idea of what the siblings have in mind

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony is forced to tackle a case with a personal element when his father is discovered drunk in a car that has a dead body in the boot. The victim is revealed to be Navy lieutenant Dean Massey, who DiNozzo Sr had threatened to kill after being cut out of a land development deal. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy captain Jake Marsden is murdered in his hotel room and inquiries lead to marine lieutenant Emma Reynolds - who tells the team she was the intended target. After being stationed in Kabul, she became pregnant by a local man called Amir who was later killed in action - and Abby discovers he was the sole male heir of a wealthy family that owns a large tract of land in a politically sensitive part of Afghanistan

programme.desc: Paige must simplify the science for the geniuses of Team Scorpion when their mental faculties are impaired during a job to prevent the creation of a black hole

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison and Ariel discover the murdered bodies of a shop owner and her daughter, and later identify two suspects on the basis of their dreams about the case, which provide them with very different perspectives on what happened. Drama, starring Patricia Arquette

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison has confusing visions about a boy while helping Devalos investigate the death of a respected congressman. Paranormal drama, starring Patricia Arquette

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: In late summer, the lifeguards tackle the antics of drunks and other disorderly revellers, while Ben 'Quiggers' Quigley enjoys a spot of Hawaiian paddle surfing. They also deal with a tsunami alert caused by an earthquake in Chile, but many people refuse to leave the beach

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An adulterous woman kills her wealthy husband, and then tries to convince the authorities his death was caused by a medical condition, implicating Dr Sloan in the process. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The wife of a comedian is killed, but evidence suggests her partner may have been the intended target. Detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke, Barry Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion heads to the bayou to track down a cagey alligator in order to stop a deadly mosquito-borne virus

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team use their high IQs to win a softball game when they are challenged by the homeland security director, with all their future government contracts on the line

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are investigated by the FBI on suspicion of murdering arms dealer La Grenouille. Jenny Shepard is the prime suspect, as she believed the criminal killed her father some years earlier, so Gibbs forms a plan to help his boss. However, an eyewitness later comes forward and implicates Tony. Starring Lauren Holly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Marine is seemingly killed in a mortar attack on America's heavily fortified administrative HQ in Baghdad, but a subsequent autopsy reveals he was murdered. Intelligence analyst Nikki Jardine volunteers to help Tony investigate the case - but her behaviour in Iraq makes him suspect she had an ulterior motive for requesting the assignment

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A dramatic undercover operation results in the death of a serial killer, seemingly bringing the case to an abrupt conclusion. However, it gradually becomes clear that he may have worked with an accomplice - and evidence emerges to implicate the new man in Ziva's life. Drama, starring Cote de Pablo and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Assistant coroner Palmer is called to the building site on a Navy base where a body has been found. While looking into the incident, he spots an armed man fleeing the scene with the victim's passport and is almost killed as he gives chase. It appears the document was stolen to hinder the investigation, but the discovery of a second corpse reveals a case of stolen identity and a compensation racket

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part story, the entire team is put in danger while hunting for the killer of a former NCIS agent. Jenny Shepard calls in Mike Franks for help, afraid she too is a target - and convinced Gibbs will be next. American crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Vance sticks rigidly to the rules as he investigates the death of a fellow agent, but Gibbs and Franks take matters into their own hands to catch the killers. The case also leads to major changes for the agency. Drama, starring Rocky Carroll, Mark Harmon and Muse Watson

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion heads to the bayou to track down a cagey alligator in order to stop a deadly mosquito-borne virus

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team use their high IQs to win a softball game when they are challenged by the homeland security director, with all their future government contracts on the line

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sam, Callen and the rest of the team make their way through Frank's list, tracking down the remaining sleeper agents hiding in America's seven largest cities. But when raids recover no nuclear bombs, they draw a blank - until a Russian arms dealer makes it known he has three nukes for sale on the black market. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval technology scientist is being rushed to hospital with a gunshot wound, only for an unknown assailant to stop the ambulance and finish the job there and then, while also killing everyone else on board. The investigation takes an intriguing turn when the post-mortem reveals an object was removed from the victim's chest, and even more so when an unlikely link emerges between the dead man and a surfer, giving Deeks and Kensi the chance to pull on their wetsuits and head down to the beach. Eric Christian Olsen and Daniela Ruah star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A millionaire former CIA man meets a premature end when someone plants a controlled explosive in his lighter. Over the years he had built up an impressive list of enemies, so finding his killer looks like an uphill task, but as the agents start working through the potential culprits, a recovered computer file leads them to an escort service used by a wanted hitman. However, what they don't bank on is the chief suspect turning up dead

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The agents are alerted to an armoured-car heist in which five men stole $70million in gold bars and killed two armed guards in the process. When Callen and the team investigate, they encounter the dead bodies of all but one of the robbers along with the entire seized haul - but is their luck too good to be true? Meanwhile, Granger partners up with a reluctant Kensi. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam, Kensi and Deeks cancel their Christmas plans and jet off to East Asia to investigate the death of a fellow NCIS special agent. They soon discover that a complicated murder has taken place and identify a pair of suspects. Meanwhile, back at headquarters, festive celebrations are getting under way as Nell and Eric make the most of the mistletoe lying around, and Hetty reveals that she is a real softie at heart. Drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: Medium

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Skelthwaite struggles to adjust to the loss of Sally Boothe, as husband Billy vows revenge for a rugby team's prank rather than face the reality of his wife's death. Anna and Kim welcome the latest recruit, excitable trainee Zoe Phelps, while the hospital staff attempt to make a young heart transplant patient's wish come true. Lesley Dunlop and Denise Van Outen star

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Matthew Kelly guest stars as factory worker Barry, whose shocking secret is revealed when Zoe goes to give a healthy eating talk at his firm. The trainee nurse also finds romance with a sexy stranger, while Harding is concerned about the arrival of an old rival in Skelthwaite. The rugby pitch gets waterlogged - so Billy persuades his team-mates to try cookery classes. Drama, with Andrea Lowe

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Samantha Janus guest stars as the wife of a man with a muscle-wasting disease, who arouses Kim's suspicions when she comes into the hospital sporting bruises. Danny struggles to cope with Cady, while Harding and David are unsure what to tell Anna about the factory's problems. Nathan worries about Billy's parenting skills, and Zoe gets more than she bargained for after organising a pub quiz team

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: David and Anna's plans for a romantic weekend away are shattered when their family and friends decide to join them, and Luke has an important question for Megan. Meanwhile, Billy faces up to Sally's death and Zoe crosses swords with feisty local Gaynor, who is intent on cleaning up the canal. Guest starring Celia Imrie

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Jack Clayton (ex-Emmerdale actor Ian Kelsey) and his family visit Skelthwaite, the home town of his wife Nisha (former Coronation Street actress Shobna Gulati), who happens to be a nurse - handy for Anna, who is struggling to replace Kim. David doesn't feel like celebrating his birthday as the factory's problems reach crisis point, and Zoe becomes suspicious of a patient's boyfriend

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Richard Sharpe becomes a pawn in a French plan to retain control of Spain, and finds himself facing execution for murder. Determined to regain his honour, he undertakes a dangerous quest to find the only person capable of clearing his name. Adaptation of Bernard Cornwell's novel, starring Sean Bean

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The death of Sharon and Tracey's Aunt Sylvie soon has them jetting off to Hollywood in search of their real father. Festive special of the comedy from 1993, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson, with guest appearances by George Hamilton, George Wendt and Richard Branson

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Part one of two. The green-fingered amateur sleuths are drawn into another mystery when a barrister employing them to spruce up his garden discovers his cousin has been drowned, only for the man to be spotted alive and well a few days later. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Part two of two. The amateur sleuths continue their investigation after Martin Frazer's cousin, who supposedly drowned, is spotted alive and well a few days later. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are enlisted to design a memorial garden based on the myth of Orpheus and Eurydice, but someone begins vandalising their work on a nightly basis. Matters are complicated still further by the death of a recovering alcoholic, whose body is found at the site. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris, with Phyllida Law, Joanna David and Daisy Dunlop

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The annual wine show is a major event in Brokenwood and no one is looking forward to it more than DI Mike, the recent buyer of a 10-acre vineyard. However, when a guest judge and wine critic are found dead, drowned inside a fermenting vat, the show takes second place to the murder investigation. Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson star

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu must track down a vital witness in a crown court trial. Mark Benton and Jo Joyner star in the comedy drama about an oddball couple of private detectives investigating criminal acts in Stratford-upon-Avon

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: An asylum seeker is murdered on a notorious Edinburgh housing estate in what appears to be a race-hate crime - but Rebus suspects there is something more complex behind it. A local MP also reports a missing Kosovan girl, who was last seen in a car with a known criminal - and it turns out he has vanished as well. Ken Stott stars as Ian Rankin's jaded detective, with Claire Price and Cora Bissett

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Three teenagers are shot dead while enjoying a picnic in a secluded wood. When a colleague of Wallander's is also murdered, he becomes convinced the two crimes are related, and sets out to catch the killer. However, the detective is faced with a lack of clues and an assassin who always seems to be one step ahead. Drama based on the best-selling books by Henning Mankell, starring Kenneth Branagh and Sarah Smart

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A diabetic girl disappears without her insulin, and Lynley discovers she was last seen talking to a couple who had previously been sighted with a youngster later found drowned in a lake. The detective stages a press appeal, hoping to learn more about the mysterious pair and reveal their roles in both cases. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The death of Sharon and Tracey's Aunt Sylvie soon has them jetting off to Hollywood in search of their real father. Festive special of the comedy from 1993, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson, with guest appearances by George Hamilton, George Wendt and Richard Branson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Suspected rapist Tom Norris stands trial but walks away from court a free man after the victim's plucky effort to give evidence crumbles under tough interrogation from Banksy's wife Naomi Woods. Sam is obsessed with nailing her suspect and picks over the case in minute detail, but forensic evidence offers no help. When a similar crime is committed at the same scene, it is Banksy who realises they must have been looking in the wrong place

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sam struggles to get a lead on Tom Norris in the rape case and new information from Naomi throws the spotlight on his housemate Dan Cox, who once covered for his friend to help him escape sexual harassment charges. The theory that the pair are working together to prey on young women seems more plausible - until Norris turns up bound and beaten in the boot of Cox's car

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A young shoplifter is found in possession of counterfeit bank notes and Gina discovers he has been badly beaten. Determined to get a lead on the mastermind behind the forgery operation, she is tempted to bend the rules while putting the lad's aunt (BBC London 94.9 presenter JoAnne Good) under surveillance. With a cameo appearance by DJ Scott Mills

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Callum realises a bank job mirrors a robbery he once solved, and pulls in his prime suspect from the old case for questioning. It emerges the villain still holds a grudge against him - not least because they were love rivals. When Callum is later sent to arrest him, he turns violent, prompting Callum to lose control and respond with unreasonable force. Gina resorts to blackmail to save her colleague's career

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar is gripped by an outbreak of dysentery, but that's the least of mum-to-be Marie Amos's worries when her husband Tony is arrested for gross indecency in the gentlemen's toilets and faces a criminal hearing. Nurse Crane meets Dolores McEvoy, a pregnant Irish woman who is clearly malnourished - but when she visits the woman's home, it turns out she has given a false address and has disappeared. Trixie is delighted when Tom asks her to oversee her first official church duty since their engagement, while at Nonnatus House, Fred tackles a rat infestation - only for Sister Monica Joan to work against him. With guest appearances by Cara Theobold (Downton Abbey) and Richard Fleeshman (Coronation Street)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Winifred's eyes are opened to Poplar's seedy underworld when she cares for expectant prostitute Bridget Cole. Horrified by the young woman's lack of awareness, she draws on her teaching experience, and with help from Sgt Noakes sets out to promote safe sex to all local working girls. Barbara struggles to understand why expectant father Frank Robbins would pressure his wife to bear him a son, while Sister Monica Joan is feeling sidelined - until she finds herself unexpectedly assisting Shelagh with an emergency birth. Trixie invites herself along to a meeting between Tom and the Bishop - but things get tense when the idea of the couple moving to Newcastle is raised

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A Christian Scientist couple refuse medicine for their newborn baby, leaving Sister Julienne questioning the nature of absolute faith. However, the case becomes more complicated when the parents are accused of harming their child. Barbara uses her initiative to communicate with a pregnant Sylheti woman who cannot speak English, although using the patient's young son to translate provides new problems. Sister Mary Cynthia returns to Nonnatus House and a woman catches handyman Fred's eye

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Secret
programme.desc: Feature-length dramatisation of the writer's only thriller, set in 19th-century Tyneside. Colin Buchanan stars as successful businessman Freddie Musgrave, who has worked hard for 20 years to live down his misspent youth as runner to a gang of smugglers and is shocked when a face appears from long ago, threatening to reveal the details of his sordid past. With Clare Higgins and June Whitfield

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Boxing Day sees heavy snowfall covering the country and the adverse weather causes major disruption across Poplar as roads become blocked and pipes freeze meaning families have to queue outside for water from standpipes. Sister Julienne must inform a family about the death of their loved one and uncovers an uncomfortable truth, while Valerie helps an unmarried couple living in a caravan prepare for their baby, but unforeseen complications knock her confidence in her ability as a midwife. Guest starring Anita Dobson

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers while holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London - a case involving a cleric whose disappearance is seemingly linked to an Irish train robbery. Feature-length mystery, starring Joan Hickson, Caroline Blakiston and Joan Greenwood

programme.title: The Casual Vacancy
programme.desc: Tensions rise in Pagford as the parish council election approaches and each side steps up its campaign, with the fate of Sweetlove House hanging in the balance. Meanwhile, Miles pines for a quiet life and promises Samantha that once the vote is over everything will calm down, but she doesn't believe him, and Howard's dark secret is revealed in the Ghost's final post. Krystal returns home to find Terri back to her old ways, Hope seems more fragile than ever and news of a tragedy leaves the whole community reeling

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Meryl's near-death experience sets Hugh on a journey of redemption and gives Penny the clarity she needs

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. As Gina evacuates the building, Smithy takes his life in his hands to disarm the ranting maniac

programme.desc: Elizabeth wants to know why she was abused as a child, and struggles to accept her mother's decision to refuse treatment. programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: William learns that getting fired in French sounds just the same as it does in English, but the Fields know they can always go back to England if the worst comes to the worst

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Geoffrey persuades Diana to see a doctor about her foot, but his suggestion of adopting a new diet doesn't go down well

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth decides to take Daddy out for a picnic, but her plans soon backfire. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Nora dons a revealing outfit for a church pageant and is dared by Ivy to call Compo's bluff and present herself on his doorstep. Unfortunately, the shock proves too much for him and he is raced to hospital. Alas, he failed to make it through, presenting his friends with a dilemma on how best to commemorate him. Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton star in a special instalment of Roy Clarke's comedy, marking the departure of Bill Owen's character from the series

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: Lighthaven is stunned when a teenage girl appears to return from the dead, leaving the town to wonder where she has been for a year and who is buried in her place

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: A grieving Luther returns to work in the wake of his ex-wife's murder, and immediately finds himself plunged into a nightmarish case. A killer wearing a Punch mask has started to stalk the streets, and as the body count rises, it becomes clear he is determined to enter folklore. However, the detective's attentions are divided as he also tries to rescue an old friend's daughter from the dangerous world of prostitution. Crime thriller, starring Idris Elba and Ruth Wilson

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A body is found, prompting two people to confess to murder. When tests show the corpse is more than 600 years old, the team must discover why the pair took responsibility for a crime they could not possibly have committed and if they are in fact the perpetrators of other killings. Alun Armstrong and James Bolam star

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Two long-dead bodies are found in a disused coal mine on the outskirts of Glasgow when it is reopened to serve as the central attraction for a museum. Investigations reveal the pair had been involved in violent encounters just before their deaths, and the search to discover their identities uncovers deeply buried resentments in the community. Detective drama, starring Alex Norton, Blythe Duff and John Michie

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gina's last day with the Sun Hill watch reaches a climax with a tense stand-off as manic stalker Jake Quinn holds Smithy and two schoolgirls at gunpoint, refusing to talk to anyone except the woman he has been harassing. Callum and Rachel keep cool heads and reconnoitre the scene, while the Armed Response Unit is summoned to bring a swift resolution to the situation, but hearts sink when a shot rings out around the school

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James and Siegfried react very differently when the Veterinary Record publishes one of Calum's articles. Rural drama based upon the novels of James Herriot, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and John McGlynn

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Burke and the team investigate university lecturer and crime novelist Mark Joffe, whose debut publication includes the detailed description of a murder identical to that of a real-life unsolved case. As the case unfolds, Burke becomes convinced of the writer's guilt, but cannot seem to find sufficient evidence to charge him. Guest starring Finlay Robertson (Life Begins), with Alex Norton, Blythe Duff and John Michie

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain heads to the West Country, picking fruit with jam maker Sarah Churchill and creating a mixed berry conserve to use in doughnuts. She also visits garlic farmer Mark Botwright and is put to work digging garlic bulbs, which inspires her recipe for apple walnut and coriander salad with burnt garlic dressing. Nadiya also puts her twist on local classics, with a tropical cream tea, and lamb and apple pasties

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bedroom maisonette in London, a house in Barrow-in-Furness, Cumbria, and a mid-terrace in Goldenhill, Stoke-on-Trent

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and the team hunt for bargains at Exeter's Westpoint Arena. Helping the red and blue teams spend £300 on three items are experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet single mum Louise, who is hoping for somewhere to grow food and her two children to indulge in their hobbies of chess and gymnastics

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps an Oxford couple find a new home on the West Sussex coast, and dons protective gear to join a conservation project protecting nature's most productive workers

programme.title: Missing Link (2019)
programme.desc: Fearless explorer Sir Lionel Frost is approached by fur-covered, eight-foot-tall creature Mr Link who, tired of living a solitary existence in the Pacific Northwest, wants to find his long-lost relatives in the fabled valley of Shangri-La. Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Jackman, Zach Galifianakis, Zoe Saldana, David Walliams and Stephen Fry

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, a mother and daughter from Swansea, and couples from Manchester and Surrey have their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ricky Wilson from the Kaiser Chiefs, as they assist a paralympic athlete and her boyfriend from Sheffield in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a chef, a life coach, and a salesman

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Special edition featuring Mike Nolan, Toyah Willcox, Dr Linda Papadopoulos, Dr Zoe Williams, Nathan Caton, Suzi Ruffell, Anthony Costa and Shaun Williamson

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: An engaged couple from Hamilton, near Glasgow, answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty heads to the clinic seeking revenge for his mother's poor treatment, accompanied by Jan posing as a potential patient - but he receives a distressing gets a call from Jade that changes everything - Bibi has been rushed into the ED after an overdose. Tina is concerned that something is not right with Jacob, who confesses his true feelings for her after a stressful day overwhelms him

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha treats the mother of the man she left standing at the altar, and who holds her responsible for the death of her husband. Fenisha's difficult situation is made even more complicated when her former fiance starts working at Holby as a locum registrar. Rash puts his medical training to the test, and Marty channels his anger into a good cause after his mother's suicide attempt

programme.title: Radio 1's Big Weekend 2021
programme.desc: Greg James introduces highlights from this year's virtual festival, including performances by Ed Sheeran, AJ Tracey and Royal Blood recorded in front of iconic backdrops around the UK. The programme also includes highlights of a special show at the BBC Radio Theatre, featuring Celeste, Mabel, Anne-Marie, Wolf Alice and London Grammar

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to count down 10 of the greatest Premier League champions

programme.title: Foxcatcher (2014)
programme.desc: A reclusive, eccentric millionaire persuades an Olympic gold-medal-winning wrestler to join his personal team. Initially flattered by the attention lavished upon him by his wealthy new friend, the grappler flourishes - but tension soon grows between them when his brother is employed as a coach, a situation that leads to tragedy. Fact-based drama, starring Steve Carell, Channing Tatum and Mark Ruffalo

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley shares the stories of a man who found religion and denounced violence, to the cathedral helping people back into the workplace

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: Celebrities of differing faiths and beliefs don walking boots to tackle the 1,000km Italian section of the ancient Via Francigena, which starts in Canterbury and finishes in Rome, where a private audience with the Pope awaits. In the first episode, actors Les Dennis and Lesley Joseph, dancer Brendan Cole, comedians Stephen K Amos and Katy Brand, athlete Greg Rutherford, singer Dana and TV host Mehreen Baig each arrive in Switzerland to find out who is sharing their trek. In just 15 days, they will walk some sections and travel some by minibus, though the final 100km will all be on foot. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Sumera Angus is used to getting her own way and firmly believes she and her family will be much happier living in Australia. They spend a week in Brisbane to see what to offer - but it may not be enough to convince cautious husband Murray. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is joined by Nick Hall and Danny Sebastian at Mona Showground on Anglesey, and pays a visit to a local jewellery maker to find out more about amber

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones explores the fascinating history of Newstead Abbey in Nottinghamshire, and YolanDa Brown meets Emerald O'Hanrahan, who plays Emma Grundy in BBC Radio 4's The Archers

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal for funds on behalf of a registered charity

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore searches for tired and unwanted items at Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey she can transform from skip-bound to stylish. With three items saved, Sarah turns to upholsterer Sharon O'Connor to overhaul an old chair frame, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph attempts to transform a dated desk. Sarah's challenge is to update a set of four chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Coco (2017)
programme.desc: A Mexican boy longs to be a guitarist, despite his family hating music. During the Day of the Dead festivities, he gets transported to the land of the dead, and with the help of a cunning skeleton, hopes to find his ancestor and learn the secrets of his family's history. Animated fantasy, with the voices of Anthony Gonzalez and Gael Garcia Bernal

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of a tiger in the dense jungles of India's Bandhavgarh Reserve. The big cat has four newborn cubs to protect and is the dominant presence in a prime hunting territory. However, rival tigers are determined to take over, and one of her own previous offspring becomes one of the most persistent enemies the mother and her cubs face

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit the Llyn Peninsula in north Wales to report on plans for a major energy project harnessing the power of the tide, discovering what local people make of the plan and assess its potential impact on wildlife and fishing. Matt also checks on the health of a critical crop of carbon-capturing sea grass, and helps launch new research into mountain goats, while Joe Crowley discovers why angel sharks are being sighted off the Welsh coast more frequently

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the grounds of Salisbury Cathedral in Wiltshire, where surprises include a mysterious object linked to the Titanic, a well-used watch with connections to early moon exploration and an undiscovered letter written by Winston Churchill. There are also items of museum quality, such as an exquisite piece of Indian textile and a prized collection of Gurkha medals

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The Nonnatus House staff struggles to come to terms with Nancy's revelation. Sister Frances and Trixie rally the others to take a stand and do something about Poplar's growing housing crisis. Sister Hilda relishes taking charge when Sister Julienne takes her annual leave, Timothy Turner prepares to leave for medical school, and Cyril is forced to reconsider his future after he receives news from home. A very special baby is born, causing a rift in a close-knit family and leading many people to count their blessings

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Past and present members of the cast and crew of the period drama celebrate the show's 10th anniversary. Featuring behind the scenes footage giving viewers an insight into how the costume, design, music, and make-up teams work to finesse every detail seen on screen, and a compilation of some of the most memorable moments from past episodes

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: Another chance to see some highlights of recent series featuring Chris Harris, Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness messing around in cars. Freddie attempts to hit 200mph in an old Jaguar and samples some of James Bond's greatest-ever cars, including the legendary Aston Martin DB5. Chris heads out on track to test Lamborghini's most powerful car ever, the hybrid Sian

programme.title: Anton Ferdinand: Football, Racism & Me
programme.desc: The former professional footballer goes on a deeply personal journey to explore the issue of racial abuse in the game, talking about his own highly-publicised 2011 incident with ex-England captain John Terry. Anton confronts how the ripple effects of the incident and the online abuse he experienced not only affected his own mental health and career but also the lives of his loved ones. He reveals where he believes players are still being failed by the system, and the ways in which football urgently needs to change

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: An engaged couple from Hamilton, near Glasgow, answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell explores a cottage in Saffron Walden, Essex, Dion Dublin visits a terrace in Manchester, and Martin Roberts looks at a plot of land near Wolverhampton

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement agents Ben Dirom and Myles Whitworth visit a car dealership to get a refund for a customer who bought an unroadworthy car, but are subjected to a barrage of abuse. In Peckham, sheriffs Luke Peacock and Grant Bailey shut down a cosmetics retailer where the owner has failed to pay his debts

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles presents footage of an angler fishing on a rock who is swept into a raging sea, and a mountain biker whose attempt at a stunt does not go according to plan

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents the show from Leominster, Herefordshire, and visits an antique doll restorer. Experts Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri help out today's teams

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore unearths three hidden gems that she believes have profit-making potential. She calls on lighting expert Guy Trench to find a new use for a collection of auction cast-offs, while designer Daniel Heath has a tallboy chest of drawers to get creative with. A flatpack dining set is given the upcycling treatment by Sarah herself

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a pair of early retirees find a new home in Wiltshire or Somerset - but it must be a period property with high ceilings and at least four bedrooms

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers help Adam and Frederique in Bracknell to turn their front garden into an upcycled, child-friendly eco paradise with vegetable beds for £3,000

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Steven Moore hunt for bargains in North Yorkshire and Tyne and Wear, acquiring a set of 18th-century tea bowls and a pair of art deco vases

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Close Shave
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated characters embark on their third adventure. Wallace falls in love with the proprietor of the local wool shop - only for Gromit to be framed for sheep rustling. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Anne Reid

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Socially awkward joke-shop owner Miranda is asked out to dinner by an old friend, prompting a visit to a clothing store to buy an outfit suitable for the date - but the expedition ends in disaster. First episode of the comedy, starring Miranda Hart, Sarah Hadland, Patricia Hodge and Sally Phillips

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: There's an incident at the gym when Linda pushes herself too hard. Iqra supports Mila as she stands up for what she believes. Kheerat asks Sharon to keep their relationship quiet in a bid to keep on Ben's good side, and Mo announces she has been invited on a cruise with Fat Elvis - but is not going because she is worried about Jean

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Mischievous Basil pretends he has forgotten about his 15th wedding anniversary - but Sybil responds by walking out on him. With friends due to arrive for a celebratory party, the harassed hotelier is forced to take desperate steps to convince them all is well on the marital front. Guest starring Una Stubbs and Ken Campbell

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: The police have made an arrest and the fragile pact is at breaking point. As Anna, Nancy, Louie and Cat are interviewed, a frustrated Max decides to resort to more unorthodox methods to get to the truth

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: A farewell Christmas dinner for Jessie is interrupted by Tom, but any hopes of reconciliation are thrown off course by an encounter with a weed brownie

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Model Steve kept his relationship with ex Luise a secret - but was that so he could play the field? After seeing a suspicious video Tia wants to know what her ex Alex really got up to at Carnival? Now face to face will she get the truth? And Lewis is desperate to find out whether his ex Lara slept with one of his mates. Will she finally come clean and could they try again? Find out as we eavesdrop on these tense, confrontational, flirty and often emotional reunions.

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The contenders put their marketing hats on, as they are given the challenge to create and market a product, including producing an infomercial in front of a green screen

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, a mother and daughter from Swansea, and couples from Manchester and Surrey have their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson introduce a collection of weird clips from the natural world, featuring a solar death ray that's frying birds mid-flight, how the sun changed the course of musical history with a violin worth 15 million dollars, and a shocking new discovery - spider goats

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Amelie wants a Queen Victoria den, where she can pass on her love of history to her friends

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Team captains Tara and Caleb battle it out, and are joined by comedians Jarred Christmas, Ola Labib and Nathan Caton, and CBBC's Naomi Wilkinson

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: From the Martin Mere Wetland Centre

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Highlights of the series in which Steve Backshall undertook a global quest from the Arctic Circle to Antarctica, encountering the world's deadliest animals in their natural habitats. Featuring the presenter's encounters with polar bears and leopard seals, his failed attempts to stand up to tornado-force winds and scorching-hot lava flows, and previously unseen footage of his bumps, trips and slips

programme.title: Human Universe
programme.desc: In episode two, Professor Brian Cox is off to India, where he assesses arguably the first evidence of rational thought in literature, the poetry of the Vedic monks. They pondered mankind's origins, realising there must have been a day with no yesterday - a day of creation - prompting the age-old question of where did the universe come from? Brian marvels that the universe seems to follow a set of rules, the laws of physics, allowing space to be considered on the grandest scale, travelling to the most distant, farthest reaches of the cosmos just by using our minds. Brian also visits Japan, and offers viewers the idea that man lives in just one of an infinite number of universes that are being made all the time

programme.title: Mountain Gorilla
programme.desc: Ageing silverback leader Titus faces his greatest challenge when he tries to care for a vulnerable orphan, while hot-headed youngster Marembo sets about making his presence felt in the mountains of Uganda. Narrated by Patrick Stewart

programme.title: Tycoon (1947)
programme.desc: A civil engineer assigned to build a railroad through the South American Andes causes friction when he refuses to work on a proposed tunnel through the mountains. He eventually gets his own way, and it looks as if he and his arrogant employer will settle their differences - until the engineer falls for his boss's daughter. Romantic drama, starring John Wayne, Cedric Hardwicke, Laraine Day and Anthony Quinn

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by Oscar-winning actor John Wayne, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career that led to him becoming considered a US national icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: A group of sea anglers from Zimbabwe chance their hand in the rolling seas off the coast of Yorkshire

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Iolo Williams tries to spot one of the most photogenic birds in the UK - the black-throated diver. Plus, live updates from Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan in Norfolk - is there any sign of the elusive turtle dove?

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon returns with the property makeover show in which cutting-edge virtual reality and visual effects enable people to see what the future of their home could look like, before it is built in reality. In the first edition architects Laura Jane Clark and Julian McIntosh compete to remodel Shelley and Steven's three-bedroom semi-detached home in Bromley, south-east London

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: Film-maker Rankin sets a series of challenges for six talented amateur photographers. In the first round, they are tested on the speed of their reactions on Brighton seafront, where they must capture stunning images using just a smart phone. They are then joined by Chris Packham in the New Forest to take shots of wildlife, and finally take a celebrity portrait of Anna Friel

programme.title: The Battle of the River Plate (1956)
programme.desc: British naval forces pursue German battleship the Admiral Graf Spee, finally blockading the vessel in Montevideo harbour, where its commanding officer is faced with an impossible choice. Second World War adventure from Powell and Pressburger, starring John Gregson, Anthony Quayle, Peter Finch, Bernard Lee and Jack Gwillim

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo discovers the origins of Harry Beck's map of the London Underground, learns about a shocking surrealist show in 1930s Piccadilly and explores the headquarters of Churchill's war cabinet

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The men head off for a weekend at a training camp, but arrive late and hungry after Mainwaring's map-reading skills are found to be somewhat lacking. They then have to capture an enemy's camp during an exercise, giving the captain a chance to earn back his men's respect. Comedy first broadcast in 1969, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1984
programme.desc: Contributors including Howard Jones, Nik Kershaw, Paul Young, Nigel Planer and Midge Ure recall the year of big hair and big tunes, when a BBC ban on Frankie Goes to Hollywood's Relax led to an embarrassing gap on the show when it reached the top of the chart. The year saw the rise of one-man acts such as Nik Kershaw and Howard Jones, while Sade brought a stripped-back soulful vibe to jazz pop. German singer Nena hit the top spot with 99 Red Balloons, while the Special AKA's Free Nelson Mandela combined a political message with an irresistible tune. The year ended with the stars of the day pooling their talents with Band Aid's charity single Do They Know It's Christmas?

programme.title: Cher at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of the singer's performances on BBC programmes, including Believe, Love and Understanding, and Walking in Memphis, as well as her first UK chart-topper I Got You Babe, recorded with ex-husband and singing partner Sonny Bono

programme.title: Moonstruck (1987)
programme.desc: An Italian-American widow agrees to marry an older man - only to fall head over heels in love with his younger brother, leaving her facing an impossible choice. Romantic comedy, starring Cher in an Oscar-winning role, with Nicolas Cage, Olympia Dukakis and Danny Aiello

programme.title: 20th Century Women (2016)
programme.desc: Dorothea Fields is a determined single mother in her mid-fifties raising her teenage son Jamie in California in 1979. At a time brimming with cultural change and rebellion, she enlists the help of two younger women to help with her son's upbringing. Comedy drama, starring Annette Bening, Elle Fanning, Greta Gerwig, Billy Crudup and Lucas Jade Zumann

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1984
programme.desc: A compilation of performances, featuring the Smiths, Duran Duran, Sade, the Weather Girls, Wham! and Bronski Beat. Further stellar appearances come from the show's debuts of iconic Americans Madonna, Miami Sound Machine and Cyndi Lauper - who runs riot in the studio. Frankie Goes to Hollywood celebrate their chart dominance of the year with one of their celebrated renditions of Two Tribes, while a less credible but arguably more memorable ditty comes courtesy of Black Lace

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin helps volunteers in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, who are hoping that their flood-prone town does not suffer another inundation before the judging commences

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don gives advice on plants to use for foliage with tropical proportions. Plus, Arit Anderson continues investigating the use of peat, and Carol Klein explores Oxford Botanic Garden's euphorbias

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Special episode devoted to community gardens and planting wildflowers, exploring their benefits both to the well-being of the people who make use of them and to the environment, with positive influences on climate change and bee populations. Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor help Bristol locals to create a community plot, and Charlotte Smith visits a blooming project in Glasgow. Sean Fletcher reports on a field to fork project involving children in Northern Ireland, and Helen Skelton meets a botanist working to preserve endangered wildflowers

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits Glen Finglas to learn why the Scottish bluebell is so precious. Arlene Stuart is at Balmoral to find out about the sickness that struck the Queen's Highland ponies, while JJ Chalmers meets the young craftsman carving art from native timber. Plus, writer and conservationist Polly Pullar reveals how she turned her smallholding into a wildlife haven

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson takes a tour of Spring's finest in the Royal Botanic Gardens, Edinburgh, showcasing riveting rhododendrons and majestic meconopsis. Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray get stuck into Beechgrove's bog and pond areas, while in sunny Joppa, George Anderson continues to show how to garden in small spaces with his summer bedding in pots

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: The chef visits a five-star hotel in Madurai, Tamil Nadu, employing local housewives for genuine home-cooked dishes and samples chettinad chicken, a dish inspired by spices brought back from both East and West by merchants. In Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, he meets India's first MasterChef winner, and marks the end of his culinary journey with a small gathering of friends at his bungalow to reveal the outcome of his search for the perfect curry

programme.title: Mary Berry's Country House Secrets
programme.desc: Mary discovers the rich history of our nation's greatest stately homes through its food, beginning with Highclere Castle, the real-life Downton Abbey, renowned as the setting for some of the most lavish and influential weekend house parties in history. She takes to the estate kitchen, where she creates a delicious spread including raspberry tartlets and gamekeeper's stew, and an elaborate menu for a grand finale dinner

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Interviews with stars of the genre

programme.title: Dunkirk (1958)
programme.desc: Soldiers are separated from their comrades-in-arms during the Dunkirk evacuation and face a terrifying journey to safety. A heroic lance corporal takes charge, aiming to lead the platoon to sanctuary while those around him panic. Second World War adventure, starring John Mills, Richard Attenborough, Robert Urquhart and Bernard Lee

programme.title: D-Day: The Last Heroes
programme.desc: Historian Dan Snow presents the first of a two-part documentary examining how two years of meticulous planning, espionage and the analysis of millions of 3D aerial photographs helped the Allied forces gain a foothold in northern France during the Second World War. He reveals how the June 1944 landings were conducted in two phases, an airborne assault and an amphibious landing. The essential element of surprise was upheld with some help from inclement weather and a comprehensive deception plan

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Elizabeth Talbot and Paul Laidlaw hunt for antiques in the Bowes Museum at Barnard Castle in Co Durham, with finds including a silver tea set and a Russian drinking cup. Plus, Paul Martin looks at the museum's extraordinary lace collection

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the explorer travels through Cornwall as its emerges from the first lockdown, discovering the impact of Covid-19 on a part of the country highly reliant on tourism. Simon discovers the ongoing impact of the collapse of the tin-mining industry in the 1980s when he visits one of Britain's poorest estates, and meets the entrepreneurs who sell homemade food on the beach who are struggling to make enough money to last the winter

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary following life on farms around Scotland. Crofters Lynn and Sandra are thrown in at the deep end when they try to buy stock at auction for the first time, while the McLeans bring in a bovine fertility expert to check on their cows' pregnancies. In Shetland, Kirsty and Aimee attempt a daring rescue mission when they realise a lamb has fallen off a steep cliff

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: A trip through the natural history archives with some of the BBC's favourite wildlife presenters as they share some of their most memorable adventures across Asia. Steve Backshall looks back on one particularly unnerving adventure in Indonesia when he and his crew were chased by a group of Komodo dragons, while Chris Packham recalls how he took photographs of the Orang Rimba people, hunter-gatherers who live in the Sumatran rainforest

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: The physicist reflects on the most challenging and intriguing questions facing science today, using material from his past programmes and the latest scientific research. He begins by looking at how far the exploration of the cosmos can go, believing that humanity is at the start of a new age of travel where space flight is on the verge of becoming routine

programme.title: Arctic with Bruce Parry
programme.desc: The adventurer experiences life among remote communities in the Arctic wilderness, beginning in the Russian territory of Siberia. Arriving during the summer solstice - a period when the region experiences continuous daylight - he spends time with horse breeders and reindeer herders, and has a go at traditional riding styles. The presenter also embarks on a quest to learn more about the importance of shamanic rituals to the native people, and asks whether their traditional way of life is in danger of being permanently wiped out by the modern world

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: With his whole operation hanging by a thread and Irene Abe getting closer, Teddy travels to Panama with Avi to track down his missing money. Alton is still trying to get through to Franklin.

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Franklin, Jerome and Louie insist on attending the funeral, but honour comes at a price. Teddy heads into the Nicaraguan jungle and finds things are even worse than he was expecting.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The day arrives for Dom to overcome the traumas of the recent past by stepping up to consultant level. But how far is he willing to go to ensure his path to power? A desperate Kian may have to betray Fletch's trust to get what he wants, while Louis bears the brunt of Max's fear over her diagnosis

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill visit Liverpool couple Tony and Nayna, who love being outdoors but find their new-build garden uninspiring and have run out of ideas to revitalise it. The pair hope that with Charlie and Lee's expertise and £4,500 budget they can secure a Caribbean theme with a modern twist, while Tony in particular dreams of a centre-piece water feature

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the competition from Heanor Antiques Centre in Derbyshire, where two teams are guided by experts Catherine Southon and Thomas Forrester as they try to find three interesting items that might generate a profit at auction on a budget of £300 each. Natasha monitors their progress and also takes a historic look at the semi-precious gemstone Blue John

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Forty Hall in Enfield, north London, where items include Chippendale tables with a hefty price tag, an original Punch and Judy set used to entertain children on the lawns of the hall, and an intriguing necklace linked to a secret society. There is also rare rock memorabilia, including a poster of Mick Jagger by Andy Warhol

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The first of the North West region heats sees Tom Parker, Paul Askew and Ellis Barrie prepare their starters for the banquet marking 140 years of the Wimbledon tennis championships. The dishes include a salad made up of 10 complex elements, and a cucumber and oyster creation, with a Great British Menu veteran judging their efforts

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: North West chefs Paul Askew, Ellis Barrie and Tom Parker produce fish-based dishes, with Ellis aiming to create the flavour of a summer barbecue without actually using one. Tom serves oysters and langoustines with a risky champagne sauce that combines both traditional British and Asian flavours, while Paul cooks turbot and oysters

programme.title: Guys and Dolls (1955)
programme.desc: A gambler accepts a bet that he cannot win the heart of a strait-laced Salvation Army member. Meanwhile, his friend has to find a secret location for an illegal dice game he is organising - while also contending with the police, a mobster and a girlfriend desperate to drag him to the altar. Musical, starring Frank Sinatra, Marlon Brando and Jean Simmons. Songs include Luck Be a Lady Tonight and Sit Down You're Rocking the Boat

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Looking at the Hollywood musical, with interviews from stars such as Fred Astaire and Julie Andrews, as well as discussion of films like Singin' in the Rain and The Sound of Music

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and David Fletcher are in Ushaw in Co Durham, seeking a selection of objects to take to auction. Catherine falls in love with a model of Muffin the Mule and David is enchanted by a limited edition book by author JK Rowling. Presenter Paul Martin learns what life was like for the students who studied at Ushaw when it was open as a Catholic college

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Four famous faces go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week, with comedians Nish Kumar and Al Murray, Radio 1 DJ Clara Amfo and broadcaster Anneka Rice the first contestants. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the weekly scores are tallied resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday, when the victor chooses between a House of Games dartboard and a branded dressing gown

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the Britain of the 1930s as he sets out on another adventure. Beginning just outside Newcastle in Jarrow, Michael uncovers the desperation that led 200 men to march 300 miles to Westminster in order to petition the government for work. The first leg of his journey ends Spennymoor, Co Durham, where he meets the son of a miner who became one of the most famous 20th-century artists of the region

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson reveals a secret passion when she prepares her dark and deviant liquorice pudding, then extols the virtues of fregola - the Sardinian sun-dried and toasted couscous - with tomato and clams. Her dinner-party guests are served butterflied leg of lamb and baby aubergines with oregano and roasted red onion, followed by meringue gelato cake with sharp raspberries and dark chocolate sauce for dessert

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by Don Warrington, Zoe Lyons, Hugh Dennis and Giovanna Fletcher, who discuss Agent Running in the Field by John le Carré and Another Life by Jodie Chapman, and also share their own favourite books of all time

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Will Foster and Lynsey Elliott compete to remodel a home in Gosport, Hampshire, that holds 20 years of family memories and a lifetime of antiques and heirlooms for soon-to-be grandparents Lisa and Judy. Angela Scanlon presents

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: It's time for the Year 6 school trip, and Julia, Liz and Meg find themselves on the coach journey from Hell with humourless school secretary Mrs Lamb and clueless teaching assistant Ms Vaughn. It all kicks off when a boy banned for bad behaviour is allowed on the bus, along with his mother Ashley, and Liz decides to play the hero. Meanwhile, Kevin takes his driving test and Amanda puts her lifestyle brand Hygge Tygge online

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Actor James is waiting for his big moment on ITV's new crime drama in a static caravan that is owned by a strange family, who clearly have something to hide. Darkly comic drama, written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Adrian Dunbar and Pauline McLynn

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor and comedian David Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Janey Godley and broadcaster the Rev Richard Coles joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Film Stars Don't Die in Liverpool (2017)
programme.desc: Real life proves to be just as dramatic off-screen as it does on it for aging Hollywood superstar Gloria Grahame and her much younger lover, Peter Turner. As their mismatched romance waxes and wanes over time, events conspire to keep them in each other's lives even when it proves to be difficult and demanding. Romantic drama, starring Jamie Bell and Annette Bening

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Special episode devoted to community gardens and planting wildflowers, exploring their benefits both to the well-being of the people who make use of them and to the environment, with positive influences on climate change and bee populations. Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor help Bristol locals to create a community plot, and Charlotte Smith visits a blooming project in Glasgow. Sean Fletcher reports on a field to fork project involving children in Northern Ireland, and Helen Skelton meets a botanist working to preserve endangered wildflowers

programme.title: Subnormal: A British Scandal
programme.desc: In the 1960s, West Indian migrants to the UK counted on providing better opportunities for their children. However, there are suggestions that before having a chance to develop intellectually, their offspring were labelled as 'stupid, difficult and disruptive' as psychologists such as Hans Eysenck and Arthur Jensen theorised that black people were genetically less intelligent than white. IQ tests were then based on these theories, with the odds stacked against children from the West Indies. Featuring interviews with key participants of the time, this documentary examines what is potentially one of the biggest scandals in the history of British education

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented time, focusing on the issues and challenges that continually dominate the headlines. University Hospital Coventry's gynaecological oncology department deals with rising referrals, a growing backlog and complex cases as the hospital's theatre capacity attempts to return to normal

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: How the Celts Saved Britain
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dan Snow unravels the mystery of the lost years of 400-800AD, looking at the demise of civilisation in Britain following the collapse of the Roman Empire, and revealing how Christianity transformed Ireland into a cultural powerhouse

programme.title: Michael Palin's New Europe
programme.desc: The presenter navigates through Ukraine and Hungary, taking in the culture and talking to locals. He travels to Budapest on the Danube, where he makes his debut on the catwalk. In Kiev he meets a Leeds market trader who has married the daughter of Ukraine's prime minister, and in Yalta he finds himself on a terrifying fairground ride

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: Louise gets far too close to Peter. unaware of the terrible truth about him lurking beneath the surface. Karina tries to find new evidence for the investigation. In Danish

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: The police take Johannes to the station, and Louise fears that the truth about her and Peter Vinge will be revealed. Peter meets his ex-wife. In Danish

programme.title: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy

programme.title: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund encounters a potentially deadly situation, but no one knows her whereabouts. Meanwhile, the mystery of the murderer's identity looks like it may be resolved, Bremer delivers a blow to Hartmann's political career, and Pernille finds herself in need of help. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund refuses to believe her superiors' insistence that the murder has been solved, and considers continuing her investigation alone. Hartmann prepares to reveal embarrassing details as Bremer makes a gesture of goodwill, while Pernille and Theis attempt a reconciliation. In Danish

programme.title: Blue Peter

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: The life and work of Oliver Postgate, the man responsible for bringing to life such classic children's TV shows as Bagpuss, Clangers, Ivor the Engine and Noggin the Nog. Contributions by the animator's family and his colleague Peter Firmin tell the stories behind the making of some of his most celebrated characters, and reveal how his creations were inspired by radical contemporary politics

programme.title: From Andy Pandy to Zebedee: The Golden Age of Children's TV
programme.desc: The story of the struggle to deliver television programmes for children, who were an often overlooked audience in the days before everything went digital. Includes interviews with pioneers of children's TV, such as Bernard Cribbins, Derek Griffiths, Janet Ellis, Johnny Ball and John Craven. Narrated by Nigel Planer

programme.title: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy

programme.title: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy

programme.title: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy

programme.title: Motherland

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: A school fundraiser is not Julia's idea of an evening out but somehow she finds herself heavily involved in order to impress a former colleague and new mum crush. Comedy about the trials and traumas of middle-class motherhood, starring Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan

programme.title: Motherland

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: While Paul's away he has the perfect solution to help out Julia - he sends his parents to help. programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Anne's new car propels her into the limelight as the mums try to get in on her new car pool, while Kevin finds himself in the new role of confidant as Amanda shares a deep secret. Liz and Julia's friendship is under fire as Liz realises she is just a 'mum' friend

programme.title: Motherland

Comedy about the trials and traumas of middle-class motherhood, starring Anna Maxwell Martin, Diane Morgan and Lucy Punch

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints mountains and a waterfall enclosed by evergreens and mossy cliffs

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the provenance of a painting with a Venetian view, trying to ascertain whether it is the work of Francesco Guardi or Michele Marieschi. Owner Nick Hopkinson inherited the painting from his great grandfather and Guardi and Marieschi are both known for their depictions of Venetian views and were contemporaries in the 18th century of Canaletto. But there's a big difference in value between them - if Nick's painting is a Marieschi it could be worth half a million pounds, if it's a Guardi it could be worth up to £10million

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge

programme.title: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy

programme.title: Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: It's Frosty Frolics Day, and Love Monster plans to spend it with Bad Idea Puppy and his cousin Good Idea Puppy, who has come to visit. Love Monster thinks that since Good Idea Puppy is the guest, she should choose which activity they do first. She decides that they should start with the ice slide. Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won't be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren't fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.

We discover how thrust and rocket fuel work, when a rocket engine blasts off from the launch pad and into space, and learn about the different parts of the rocket and what they are for. Next, we find out about the special computers on-board that guide astronauts through space, and Maddie rides a very fast rollercoaster to show us how G-force works when a rocket launches. Finally, we find out how a rocket lands safely to bring the astronauts back to Earth.

Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: A half-asleep Love Monster exits his house and walks towards the green, passing snoozing citizens on the way. He notices that the whole of the green is covered with litter and snoring Fluffytowners. Love Monster remembers that yesterday was Woo Hoo Day and the whole of Fluffytown had a big party - which makes today the Day After Woo Hoo Day. Love Monster attempts to clean up all of the mess on his own as a nice surprise for his friends, but he struggles and soon realises that it is a very big job. He should ask for help! He wakes everyone up, saying if everyone picks up one piece of litter each, they can tidy up the green in no time. Elder Kitten says he knows just the song for the job. Love Monster and Elder Kitten lead the Fluffytowners in singing a funky, tidying up song as they each pick up a piece of litter to clean the green.
