Inside Nigel’s bag are a toy pig, a toy sheep and a toy chicken, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are themed around things you might find at a farm. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate farm objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie visits a robot workshop where they design farming robots that help farmers grow crops. She meets three farming robots, Tom, Wilma and Richard that work together on the farm to help find the weeds in the fields. Maddie learns that Tom, a scanning robot, can see things we cannot see, using special cameras to scan and take photos of the weeds, animals, insects and crops in the field. Maddie then discovers how Tom sends pictures and information to an artificial intelligence computer called Wilma to build a map of the field. Finally, Maddie meets another Richard, a robot who uses the map to find and destroy the weeds. Maddie then visits a printing workshop to see how a 3D printer makes things. At the workshop, they design glasses frames to wear and Maddie uses her special camera to see how the 3D printing works by building up layers stacked on top of each other.

programme.desc: The dog and the Squirrels go off on an underwater adventure with Betty's dad in his submarine, spotting creatures including a giant sea stallion and some jazz fish

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: A freshly baked apple pie is swiped from Duggee's windowsill, so he dons his detective badge to track down the thief

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey, Bingo and Mum are enjoying playing Keepy Uppy around the house with their last balloon, when Dad joins in and the game becomes the ultimate test of skill.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: When Grim HQ's heating gets stuck on, Glitch looks for a cold place to land and cool down. Meanwhile, the Go Jetters enjoy the cool sights of Harbin Ice City in China. Every year sculptors at the festival create realistic models of world famous landmarks from blocks of ice and Lars is attempting to carve the Eiffel Tower

programme.title: Jennie's Fitness in 5

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Justin, Robert and Little Monster try to look after a giant and a rather naughty plant for Mr Bloom

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘rain’ and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. This episode is all about rain – drops of rain, trickly rain, pitter-patter rain, rainbow rain and really big splasher rain! We see Dee and a friend catching rain and spot Dee singing in the rain! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug have a power cut in the bookshop. They want to know what it was like before electricity, so they travel to an old lighthouse. But before the keeper can return, the light threatens to go out! Our heroes manage to save the day with their head torches.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: A three-year-old bunny meets friends and experiences emotions as he engages with the world around him

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The friends see a woman putting coloured cardboard stars next to items in a shop window display, and get carried away putting them next to everything in sight

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Using a sign language called Makaton, Something Special helps its audience learn how to communicate as well as develop their language in a fun and exciting way. Justin meets some musical friends and they put on a concert. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is the judge of Tumbleton's Got Talent! starring Grandad Tumble, Lord Tumble and Aunt Polly

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies go on a very special picnic together. Lots of Tubby Toast pops out of the Dup Dup and lands on the picnic plates for the Teletubbies to munch

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Sullivan, Araiya, River, Taiba, Luna and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Nigel has got a shopping basket inside his bag. All the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are themed around a trip to the shops too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide a baby-appropriate shopping-themed toy or object inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, to discover with their baby! Some viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring shopping too. The fun children’s song Shopping Day features, as does a story about Baby Bear at the shops. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is sewing and makes a snazzy poncho, inspiring the squirrels to make one too. When they hear a very loud sneeze, the group investigates and finds a polar bear with a cold

programme.title: My Petsaurus
programme.desc: Fluffy joins in with Megan's game of shadow puppets

programme.title: Kiri & Lou
programme.desc: Moa’s chick is reluctant to emerge from her egg, so Kiri sings an encouraging song.

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Happy-Sad Klomp's mood changes with the weather. The Happy-Sad Klom dances when it is sunny and cries when it is cloudy. And when it snows, it is really upset! B.O.T. has to be quick to keep clear of the giant beastie's klompy feet and collect all the data.

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Fishing lessons take all forms. Mum makes it look easy but the fish slip from the grasp of young bears. Oriental darters practice with twigs and leaves. There's not a fish in sight. River otter pups watch as mum's sat nav guides her to the fish

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the town to a place where they can donate books. On the way, they discover a tree stump that looks like an octopus, spot a big red bridge, answer the Rambler phone and even hop like mermaids!

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: A brand new series of Magic Hands takes a look at the iconic songs that represent the varied music of Black artists from Nina Simone's Young, Gifted and Black to Bob Marley's Three Little Birds at various locations around the UK with links to Black history. Today, presenters Asnath and Semhar are in Notting Hill - home of the famous Notting Hill Carnival, celebrating Caribbean culture with Dancing In The Street by Martha and the Vandellas

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb is convinced there is a monster on the loose after discovering large footprints in the snow

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join nine-month-old Bonnie and Harry as they discover the world around them and become more expressive. Both children love playing with their families - laughing, smiling, clapping, playing peek-a-boo and making noise. Bonnie gets excited when her dad suggests a day out to the beach! When they get there, Bonnie is concentrating on making sandcastles before splashing her feet in the water, laughing and loving the sea. Harry and his family go on a bug hunt at a nature reserve, but what will they find? While the family enjoys a relaxing picnic, Harry tries a banana for the very first time. Bonnie is happily crawling along the beach finding seashells before mum lays out a picnic with yummy hummus and pitta bread. Before leaving, there is much laughter as they decide to try and completely cover dad with sand. After their picnic, Harry’s decides to join his brother in having his photo taken, and it’s big smiles all around!

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Woolly has to explain that the sand belongs to the beach and the sea when Tig says she wants to take her sandcastle home with her

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Valentina makes a Greek Vegetarian Moussaka for her friends because her family is from Greece

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: t's Waffle's birthday and Mr Willow throws a huge party for him. As the Brooklyn-Bells prepare a surprise for Waffle, Connie and Ruby's dog, Jelly, leads the Wonder Dog astray

programme.title: Molly and Mack

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The little boy hears a song on the radio and wants to hear it again, but he cannot make it 'come back'. The Book Animals try to help him find a way to return the tune

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘rain’ and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects that are associated with the colour gold, from treasure to golden sand, and explore how gold metal is used. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour gold.

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: Firefighter Karen shows us her fabulous helmet. We see how important it is for Karen to wear it while she does her heroic job, as well as some of the amazing things it does.

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: A journey back in time to see how dinosaurs lived, utilising the latest digital CGI and green-screen technology. In the first edition, presenter Andy Day accidentally breaks a piece of pumice stone at the museum, so he tries to get some more by travelling back in time to the age of the Tyrannosaurus Rex

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate goes for a drive in the car. She uses a special microscope camera to find out how car brakes work. An animation shows how the brake pedal is connected to the brake discs and pads and how they move to slow down and stop the car. Next, Maddie visits a factory to see how a car is made from a large roll of steel sheeting. She uses special cameras to show how robots work with people to put the different parts of the cars together

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey, Bingo and Mum are enjoying playing Keepy Uppy around the house with their last balloon, when Dad joins in and the game becomes the ultimate test of skill.

programme.title: Go Jetters

programme.title: Molly and Mack

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Animated fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When Gran Gran and JoJo find a family recipe for Great Gran Gran's signature Saint Lucian banana bread, they're excited to bake a batch of it! But there's one problem - cake mix has been spilt on the recipe, so they don't know how long to bake it for!

programme.title: Our Black History Heroes
programme.desc: Talia tells the story of the pioneering and courageous nurse and businesswoman Mary Seacole

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Tig and her Dad go on a day out on a steam train and enjoy it so much that Tig does not want it to end, leaving Woolly to explain the best days can still be remembered

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The new table Major has made, collapses, spilling soup everywhere, but when Tiny and Small fetch some more soup, the trolley crashes into a new bump in the path to the soup wells

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines look for the perfect place to have a picnic, and invite the Wottingers to join in the fun

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Olympic gold medal-winning heptathlete Jessica Ennis-Hill reads Brian Moses' The Frog Olympics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Bryn Williams and Liv Burt, plus guest Michael Ball. Helen McGinn joins via video link and picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain heads to the West Country, picking fruit with jam maker Sarah Churchill and creating a mixed berry conserve to use in doughnuts. She also visits garlic farmer Mark Botwright and is put to work digging garlic bulbs, which inspires her recipe for apple walnut and coriander salad with burnt garlic dressing. Nadiya also puts her twist on local classics, with a tropical cream tea, and lamb and apple pasties

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bedroom maisonette in London, a house in Barrow-in-Furness, Cumbria, and a mid-terrace in Goldenhill, Stoke-on-Trent

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and the team hunt for bargains at Exeter's Westpoint Arena. Helping the red and blue teams spend £300 on three items are experts Nick Hall and Richard Madley

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet single mum Louise, who is hoping for somewhere to grow food and her two children to indulge in their hobbies of chess and gymnastics

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps an Oxford couple find a new home on the West Sussex coast, and dons protective gear to join a conservation project protecting nature's most productive workers

programme.title: Missing Link (2019)
programme.desc: Fearless explorer Sir Lionel Frost is approached by fur-covered, eight-foot-tall creature Mr Link who, tired of living a solitary existence in the Pacific Northwest, wants to find his long-lost relatives in the fabled valley of Shangri-La. Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Jackman, Zach Galifianakis, Zoe Saldana, David Walliams and Stephen Fry

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, a mother and daughter from Swansea, and couples from Manchester and Surrey have their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Ricky Wilson from the Kaiser Chiefs, as they assist a paralympic athlete and her boyfriend from Sheffield in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a chef, a life coach, and a salesman

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Special edition featuring Mike Nolan, Toyah Willcox, Dr Linda Papadopoulos, Dr Zoe Williams, Nathan Caton, Suzi Ruffell, Anthony Costa and Shaun Williamson

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: An engaged couple from Hamilton, near Glasgow, answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty heads to the clinic seeking revenge for his mother's poor treatment, accompanied by Jan posing as a potential patient - but he receives a distressing gets a call from Jade that changes everything - Bibi has been rushed into the ED after an overdose. Tina is concerned that something is not right with Jacob, who confesses his true feelings for her after a stressful day overwhelms him

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha treats the mother of the man she left standing at the altar, and who holds her responsible for the death of her husband. Fenisha's difficult situation is made even more complicated when her former fiance starts working at Holby as a locum registrar. Rash puts his medical training to the test, and Marty channels his anger into a good cause after his mother's suicide attempt

programme.title: Radio 1's Big Weekend 2021
programme.desc: Greg James introduces highlights from this year's virtual festival, including performances by Ed Sheeran, AJ Tracey and Royal Blood recorded in front of iconic backdrops around the UK. The programme also includes highlights of a special show at the BBC Radio Theatre, featuring Celeste, Mabel, Anne-Marie, Wolf Alice and London Grammar

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to count down 10 of the greatest Premier League champions

programme.title: Foxcatcher (2014)
programme.desc: A reclusive, eccentric millionaire persuades an Olympic gold-medal-winning wrestler to join his personal team. Initially flattered by the attention lavished upon him by his wealthy new friend, the grappler flourishes - but tension soon grows between them when his brother is employed as a coach, a situation that leads to tragedy. Fact-based drama, starring Steve Carell, Channing Tatum and Mark Ruffalo

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley shares the stories of a man who found religion and denounced violence, to the cathedral helping people back into the workplace

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: Celebrities of differing faiths and beliefs don walking boots to tackle the 1,000km Italian section of the ancient Via Francigena, which starts in Canterbury and finishes in Rome, where a private audience with the Pope awaits. In the first episode, actors Les Dennis and Lesley Joseph, dancer Brendan Cole, comedians Stephen K Amos and Katy Brand, athlete Greg Rutherford, singer Dana and TV host Mehreen Baig each arrive in Switzerland to find out who is sharing their trek. In just 15 days, they will walk some sections and travel some by minibus, though the final 100km will all be on foot. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Sumera Angus is used to getting her own way and firmly believes she and her family will be much happier living in Australia. They spend a week in Brisbane to see what to offer - but it may not be enough to convince cautious husband Murray. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is joined by Nick Hall and Danny Sebastian at Mona Showground on Anglesey, and pays a visit to a local jewellery maker to find out more about amber

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones explores the fascinating history of Newstead Abbey in Nottinghamshire, and YolanDa Brown meets Emerald O'Hanrahan, who plays Emma Grundy in BBC Radio 4's The Archers

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal for funds on behalf of a registered charity

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore searches for tired and unwanted items at Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey she can transform from skip-bound to stylish. With three items saved, Sarah turns to upholsterer Sharon O'Connor to overhaul an old chair frame, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph attempts to transform a dated desk. Sarah's challenge is to update a set of four chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Coco (2017)
programme.desc: A Mexican boy longs to be a guitarist, despite his family hating music. During the Day of the Dead festivities, he gets transported to the land of the dead, and with the help of a cunning skeleton, hopes to find his ancestor and learn the secrets of his family's history. Animated fantasy, with the voices of Anthony Gonzalez and Gael Garcia Bernal

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: Iolo Williams continues his exploration of the Cambrian mountains, heading to the Tywi forest to look for goshawks. He tracks these birds of prey during the late part of the nesting season and finds fully grown chicks in a huge nest with bits of eaten prey below. Iolo also explores the Elan Valley to find the curlew, one of Wales' most critically endangered bird species, as they attempt to breed on rough ground near the reservoirs. And he discovers a stunning day-flying moth called the Welsh clearwing

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway walks to West Dale Bay, starting at the Dale Peninsula in South Pembrokeshire, where he learns about an invasion that changed the course of history. He also helps carve a seahorse and takes part in a cliff rescue - as the casualty. Wildlife expert Lizzie Daly gets a close look at the birds who visit the area and Onkar Singh Purewal explores how walking can aid general wellbeing

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit the Llyn Peninsula in north Wales to report on plans for a major energy project harnessing the power of the tide, discovering what local people make of the plan and assess its potential impact on wildlife and fishing. Matt also checks on the health of a critical crop of carbon-capturing sea grass, and helps launch new research into mountain goats, while Joe Crowley discovers why angel sharks are being sighted off the Welsh coast more frequently

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the grounds of Salisbury Cathedral in Wiltshire, where surprises include a mysterious object linked to the Titanic, a well-used watch with connections to early moon exploration and an undiscovered letter written by Winston Churchill. There are also items of museum quality, such as an exquisite piece of Indian textile and a prized collection of Gurkha medals

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The Nonnatus House staff struggles to come to terms with Nancy's revelation. Sister Frances and Trixie rally the others to take a stand and do something about Poplar's growing housing crisis. Sister Hilda relishes taking charge when Sister Julienne takes her annual leave, Timothy Turner prepares to leave for medical school, and Cyril is forced to reconsider his future after he receives news from home. A very special baby is born, causing a rift in a close-knit family and leading many people to count their blessings

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Past and present members of the cast and crew of the period drama celebrate the show's 10th anniversary. Featuring behind the scenes footage giving viewers an insight into how the costume, design, music, and make-up teams work to finesse every detail seen on screen, and a compilation of some of the most memorable moments from past episodes

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: Another chance to see some highlights of recent series featuring Chris Harris, Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness messing around in cars. Freddie attempts to hit 200mph in an old Jaguar and samples some of James Bond's greatest-ever cars, including the legendary Aston Martin DB5. Chris heads out on track to test Lamborghini's most powerful car ever, the hybrid Sian

programme.title: Anton Ferdinand: Football, Racism & Me
programme.desc: The former professional footballer goes on a deeply personal journey to explore the issue of racial abuse in the game, talking about his own highly-publicised 2011 incident with ex-England captain John Terry. Anton confronts how the ripple effects of the incident and the online abuse he experienced not only affected his own mental health and career but also the lives of his loved ones. He reveals where he believes players are still being failed by the system, and the ways in which football urgently needs to change

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: An engaged couple from Hamilton, near Glasgow, answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell explores a cottage in Saffron Walden, Essex, Dion Dublin visits a terrace in Manchester, and Martin Roberts looks at a plot of land near Wolverhampton

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement agents Ben Dirom and Myles Whitworth visit a car dealership to get a refund for a customer who bought an unroadworthy car, but are subjected to a barrage of abuse. In Peckham, sheriffs Luke Peacock and Grant Bailey shut down a cosmetics retailer where the owner has failed to pay his debts

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles presents footage of an angler fishing on a rock who is swept into a raging sea, and a mountain biker whose attempt at a stunt does not go according to plan

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents the show from Leominster, Herefordshire, and visits an antique doll restorer. Experts Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri help out today's teams

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore unearths three hidden gems that she believes have profit-making potential. She calls on lighting expert Guy Trench to find a new use for a collection of auction cast-offs, while designer Daniel Heath has a tallboy chest of drawers to get creative with. A flatpack dining set is given the upcycling treatment by Sarah herself

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a pair of early retirees find a new home in Wiltshire or Somerset - but it must be a period property with high ceilings and at least four bedrooms

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers help Adam and Frederique in Bracknell to turn their front garden into an upcycled, child-friendly eco paradise with vegetable beds for £3,000

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Steven Moore hunt for bargains in North Yorkshire and Tyne and Wear, acquiring a set of 18th-century tea bowls and a pair of art deco vases

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Close Shave
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated characters embark on their third adventure. Wallace falls in love with the proprietor of the local wool shop - only for Gromit to be framed for sheep rustling. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Anne Reid

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: X-Ray investigates the huge growth in thefts of catalytic converters from cars, and Mo Jannah finds out about the takeaway food services being run from people's homes.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: There's an incident at the gym when Linda pushes herself too hard. Iqra supports Mila as she stands up for what she believes. Kheerat asks Sharon to keep their relationship quiet in a bid to keep on Ben's good side, and Mo announces she has been invited on a cruise with Fat Elvis - but is not going because she is worried about Jean

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Mischievous Basil pretends he has forgotten about his 15th wedding anniversary - but Sybil responds by walking out on him. With friends due to arrive for a celebratory party, the harassed hotelier is forced to take desperate steps to convince them all is well on the marital front. Guest starring Una Stubbs and Ken Campbell

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: The police have made an arrest and the fragile pact is at breaking point. As Anna, Nancy, Louie and Cat are interviewed, a frustrated Max decides to resort to more unorthodox methods to get to the truth

programme.title: Elis James: Football Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian enjoys a fan's-eye journey through the highs and lows of Welsh footballing history in the company of some of the game's best-known players, managers and supporters

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: A farewell Christmas dinner for Jessie is interrupted by Tom, but any hopes of reconciliation are thrown off course by an encounter with a weed brownie

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Model Steve kept his relationship with ex Luise a secret - but was that so he could play the field? After seeing a suspicious video Tia wants to know what her ex Alex really got up to at Carnival? Now face to face will she get the truth? And Lewis is desperate to find out whether his ex Lara slept with one of his mates. Will she finally come clean and could they try again? Find out as we eavesdrop on these tense, confrontational, flirty and often emotional reunions.

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The contenders put their marketing hats on, as they are given the challenge to create and market a product, including producing an infomercial in front of a green screen

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, a mother and daughter from Swansea, and couples from Manchester and Surrey have their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain heads to the West Country, picking fruit with jam maker Sarah Churchill and creating a mixed berry conserve to use in doughnuts. programme.title: I Can See Your Voice

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities

programme.title: The Wall

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty heads to the clinic seeking revenge for his mother's poor treatment, accompanied by Jan posing as a potential patient - but he receives a distressing gets a call from Jade that changes everything - Bibi has been rushed into the ED after an overdose. Tina is concerned that something is not right with Jacob, who confesses his true feelings for her after a stressful day overwhelms him

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha treats the mother of the man she left standing at the altar, and who holds her responsible for the death of her husband. Fenisha's difficult situation is made even more complicated when her former fiance starts working at Holby as a locum registrar. Rash puts his medical training to the test, and Marty channels his anger into a good cause after his mother's suicide attempt

programme.title: Radio 1's Big Weekend 2021
programme.desc: Greg James introduces highlights from this year's virtual festival, including performances by Ed Sheeran, AJ Tracey and Royal Blood recorded in front of iconic backdrops around the UK. The programme also includes highlights of a special show at the BBC Radio Theatre, featuring Celeste, Mabel, Anne-Marie, Wolf Alice and London Grammar

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to count down 10 of the greatest Premier League champions

programme.title: Foxcatcher (2014)
programme.desc: A reclusive, eccentric millionaire persuades an Olympic gold-medal-winning wrestler to join his personal team. Initially flattered by the attention lavished upon him by his wealthy new friend, the grappler flourishes - but tension soon grows between them when his brother is employed as a coach, a situation that leads to tragedy. Fact-based drama, starring Steve Carell, Channing Tatum and Mark Ruffalo

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley shares the stories of a man who found religion and denounced violence, to the cathedral helping people back into the workplace

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: Sam Nwokoro and Victoria Walker, both students at New College, University of Edinburgh, lead this week's Reflections

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Sumera Angus is used to getting her own way and firmly believes she and her family will be much happier living in Australia. They spend a week in Brisbane to see what to offer - but it may not be enough to convince cautious husband Murray. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is joined by Nick Hall and Danny Sebastian at Mona Showground on Anglesey, and pays a visit to a local jewellery maker to find out more about amber

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones explores the fascinating history of Newstead Abbey in Nottinghamshire, and YolanDa Brown meets Emerald O'Hanrahan, who plays Emma Grundy in BBC Radio 4's The Archers

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal for funds on behalf of a registered charity

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore searches for tired and unwanted items at Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey she can transform from skip-bound to stylish. With three items saved, Sarah turns to upholsterer Sharon O'Connor to overhaul an old chair frame, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph attempts to transform a dated desk. Sarah's challenge is to update a set of four chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Coco (2017)
programme.desc: A Mexican boy longs to be a guitarist, despite his family hating music. During the Day of the Dead festivities, he gets transported to the land of the dead, and with the help of a cunning skeleton, hopes to find his ancestor and learn the secrets of his family's history. Animated fantasy, with the voices of Anthony Gonzalez and Gael Garcia Bernal

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of a tiger in the dense jungles of India's Bandhavgarh Reserve. The big cat has four newborn cubs to protect and is the dominant presence in a prime hunting territory. However, rival tigers are determined to take over, and one of her own previous offspring becomes one of the most persistent enemies the mother and her cubs face

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit the Llyn Peninsula in north Wales to report on plans for a major energy project harnessing the power of the tide, discovering what local people make of the plan and assess its potential impact on wildlife and fishing. Matt also checks on the health of a critical crop of carbon-capturing sea grass, and helps launch new research into mountain goats, while Joe Crowley discovers why angel sharks are being sighted off the Welsh coast more frequently

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the grounds of Salisbury Cathedral in Wiltshire, where surprises include a mysterious object linked to the Titanic, a well-used watch with connections to early moon exploration and an undiscovered letter written by Winston Churchill. There are also items of museum quality, such as an exquisite piece of Indian textile and a prized collection of Gurkha medals

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The Nonnatus House staff struggles to come to terms with Nancy's revelation. Sister Frances and Trixie rally the others to take a stand and do something about Poplar's growing housing crisis. Sister Hilda relishes taking charge when Sister Julienne takes her annual leave, Timothy Turner prepares to leave for medical school, and Cyril is forced to reconsider his future after he receives news from home. A very special baby is born, causing a rift in a close-knit family and leading many people to count their blessings

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Past and present members of the cast and crew of the period drama celebrate the show's 10th anniversary. Featuring behind the scenes footage giving viewers an insight into how the costume, design, music, and make-up teams work to finesse every detail seen on screen, and a compilation of some of the most memorable moments from past episodes

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: Another chance to see some highlights of recent series featuring Chris Harris, Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness messing around in cars. Freddie attempts to hit 200mph in an old Jaguar and samples some of James Bond's greatest-ever cars, including the legendary Aston Martin DB5. Chris heads out on track to test Lamborghini's most powerful car ever, the hybrid Sian

programme.title: Anton Ferdinand: Football, Racism & Me
programme.desc: The former professional footballer goes on a deeply personal journey to explore the issue of racial abuse in the game, talking about his own highly-publicised 2011 incident with ex-England captain John Terry. Anton confronts how the ripple effects of the incident and the online abuse he experienced not only affected his own mental health and career but also the lives of his loved ones. He reveals where he believes players are still being failed by the system, and the ways in which football urgently needs to change

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: An engaged couple from Hamilton, near Glasgow, answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell explores a cottage in Saffron Walden, Essex, Dion Dublin visits a terrace in Manchester, and Martin Roberts looks at a plot of land near Wolverhampton

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement agents Ben Dirom and Myles Whitworth visit a car dealership to get a refund for a customer who bought an unroadworthy car, but are subjected to a barrage of abuse. In Peckham, sheriffs Luke Peacock and Grant Bailey shut down a cosmetics retailer where the owner has failed to pay his debts

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles presents footage of an angler fishing on a rock who is swept into a raging sea, and a mountain biker whose attempt at a stunt does not go according to plan

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents the show from Leominster, Herefordshire, and visits an antique doll restorer. Experts Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri help out today's teams

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore unearths three hidden gems that she believes have profit-making potential. She calls on lighting expert Guy Trench to find a new use for a collection of auction cast-offs, while designer Daniel Heath has a tallboy chest of drawers to get creative with. A flatpack dining set is given the upcycling treatment by Sarah herself

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a pair of early retirees find a new home in Wiltshire or Somerset - but it must be a period property with high ceilings and at least four bedrooms

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers help Adam and Frederique in Bracknell to turn their front garden into an upcycled, child-friendly eco paradise with vegetable beds for £3,000

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Steven Moore hunt for bargains in North Yorkshire and Tyne and Wear, acquiring a set of 18th-century tea bowls and a pair of art deco vases

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Close Shave
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated characters embark on their third adventure. Wallace falls in love with the proprietor of the local wool shop - only for Gromit to be framed for sheep rustling. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Anne Reid

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: The judges Anna Campbell-Jones, Michael Angus and Kate Spiers head to the Highlands to consider a new build in Aviemore inspired by Charles Rennie Mackintosh and two historic homes in Inverness and the Beauly Firth

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: Celebrities of differing faiths and beliefs don walking boots to tackle the 1,000km Italian section of the ancient Via Francigena, which starts in Canterbury and finishes in Rome, where a private audience with the Pope awaits. In the first episode, actors Les Dennis and Lesley Joseph, dancer Brendan Cole, comedians Stephen K Amos and Katy Brand, athlete Greg Rutherford, singer Dana and TV host Mehreen Baig each arrive in Switzerland to find out who is sharing their trek. In just 15 days, they will walk some sections and travel some by minibus, though the final 100km will all be on foot. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.desc: The weather presenter and journalist examines how climate shapes everyone's lives and looks at the activities that it enables people to participate in

programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell explores a cottage in Saffron Walden, Essex, Dion Dublin visits a terrace in Manchester, and Martin Roberts looks at a plot of land near Wolverhampton

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement agents Ben Dirom and Myles Whitworth visit a car dealership to get a refund for a customer who bought an unroadworthy car, but are subjected to a barrage of abuse. In Peckham, sheriffs Luke Peacock and Grant Bailey shut down a cosmetics retailer where the owner has failed to pay his debts

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles presents footage of an angler fishing on a rock who is swept into a raging sea, and a mountain biker whose attempt at a stunt does not go according to plan

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents the show from Leominster, Herefordshire, and visits an antique doll restorer. Experts Kate Bliss and Ochucko Ojiri help out today's teams

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore unearths three hidden gems that she believes have profit-making potential. She calls on lighting expert Guy Trench to find a new use for a collection of auction cast-offs, while designer Daniel Heath has a tallboy chest of drawers to get creative with. A flatpack dining set is given the upcycling treatment by Sarah herself

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a pair of early retirees find a new home in Wiltshire or Somerset - but it must be a period property with high ceilings and at least four bedrooms

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers help Adam and Frederique in Bracknell to turn their front garden into an upcycled, child-friendly eco paradise with vegetable beds for £3,000

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Steven Moore hunt for bargains in North Yorkshire and Tyne and Wear, acquiring a set of 18th-century tea bowls and a pair of art deco vases

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Close Shave
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated characters embark on their third adventure. Wallace falls in love with the proprietor of the local wool shop - only for Gromit to be framed for sheep rustling. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Anne Reid

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Barra's Return of the Wild
programme.desc: Barra Best reveals how two islands just off Dublin Port have become home to one of Europe's most endangered breeding birds, and scientists in Scotland monitor the world's second largest fish. Plus, simple changes in a small Swiss town that have benefited nocturnal creatures, and how the development of the Amsterdam skyline has made it a new home for a creature that spends nine months of the year in the air

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: There's an incident at the gym when Linda pushes herself too hard. Iqra supports Mila as she stands up for what she believes. Kheerat asks Sharon to keep their relationship quiet in a bid to keep on Ben's good side, and Mo announces she has been invited on a cruise with Fat Elvis - but is not going because she is worried about Jean

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Mischievous Basil pretends he has forgotten about his 15th wedding anniversary - but Sybil responds by walking out on him. With friends due to arrive for a celebratory party, the harassed hotelier is forced to take desperate steps to convince them all is well on the marital front. Guest starring Una Stubbs and Ken Campbell

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: The police have made an arrest and the fragile pact is at breaking point. As Anna, Nancy, Louie and Cat are interviewed, a frustrated Max decides to resort to more unorthodox methods to get to the truth

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: The Moycraig Young Farmers prepare to take their annual three act play on tour across the country, performing scripts that have been passed down through generations - this year, it is Married Bliss by Sam Cree. The programme follows them through rehearsals and set construction, and reveals why these performances form an important part of their Ulster-Scots heritage

programme.title: BBC Arts NI Presents
programme.desc: Gemma Bradley hosts outstanding live performances from emerging home-grown musical talent. Featuring performances from Amy Montgomery, Joshua Burnside, JyellowL, Lilla Vargen, the Florentinas and Dark Tropics

programme.title: Community Life
programme.desc: A look at the work of the MS Society in Northern Ireland, a beach outing to Portrush and local research on rare diseases

programme.title: Starstruck
programme.desc: A farewell Christmas dinner for Jessie is interrupted by Tom, but any hopes of reconciliation are thrown off course by an encounter with a weed brownie

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Model Steve kept his relationship with ex Luise a secret - but was that so he could play the field? After seeing a suspicious video Tia wants to know what her ex Alex really got up to at Carnival? Now face to face will she get the truth? And Lewis is desperate to find out whether his ex Lara slept with one of his mates. Will she finally come clean and could they try again? Find out as we eavesdrop on these tense, confrontational, flirty and often emotional reunions.

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The contenders put their marketing hats on, as they are given the challenge to create and market a product, including producing an infomercial in front of a green screen

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Under cover of Covid, the Amazon rainforest is under attack. Deforestation is at levels not seen for more than a decade. Brazilian President, Jair Bolsonaro, talks of opening up the forest to development while the environmental police is under attack from loggers. Our World has obtained a recording of the Environment minister talking about using the cover of Covid to 'change all the rules' in the Amazon. For Our World, Justin Rowlatt, is on a mission to find out how a tribe he visited a decade ago is faring the face of this assault

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef's culinary tour of France leads him to the Camargue, a distinct and diverse region located south of Arles between the Mediterranean and the two arms of the Rhone delta. James begins his exploration at Les Marquises, the Laurent family's farm, where he learns all about the Camargue bull. He then barbeques a local fish, which he serves with squash chutney for the family. Later, Michelin-starred chef Armand Arnal shows James around his impressive gardens and lets him pick ingredients for his next dish - stuffed courgette flowers

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Hidden Caribbean: Havana to Haiti
programme.desc: Part two of two. Joanna completes her Cuban journey by visiting the infamous town of Guantanamo. Giving the prison a wide berth, she discovers that the song Guantanamera was born in the town, and meets a talented young singer who is recording the track herself. She then heads across the Windward Passage to Haiti, currently the poorest country in the Western World. She visits the Citadel, a mighty fortress built to deter colonial powers, before taking in a Voodoo ceremony on the Iles a Vaches

programme.title: When English Football Ruled Europe

Giving the prison a wide berth, she discovers that the song Guantanamera was born in the town, and meets a talented young singer who is recording the track herself. She then heads across the Windward Passage to Haiti, currently the poorest country in the Western World. She visits the Citadel, a mighty fortress built to deter colonial powers, before taking in a Voodoo ceremony on the Iles a Vaches

programme.desc: Steve and Snot use a cloning machine to produce two women who will agree to date them, having been thwarted in their attempts to pair up with a couple of girls for the upcoming high-school prom. Meanwhile, there are disastrous consequences when Stan takes home the CIA's cloned pet, Daren the Dodo

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna decides to run for a place on the school board and keeps her husband away from the campaign in case he embarrasses her. Junior discovers he has a natural talent as a diver, but his chances of success are threatened when he is too shy to wear the school's tight swimming trunks

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: The Browns pack their bags and move to Los Angeles, where Cleveland plans to make his dream of being a baseball scout a reality. However, his new job fails to live up to expectations, and when he befriends struggling actress Gina (guest star Krysten Ritter), he discovers that life in California is not quite as glamorous as it seems. Meanwhile, Donna launches a new career as a children's entertainer

programme.title: The Stand Up Sketch Show
programme.desc: Routines by comedians including Kojo Anim, Tessa Coates, Luke McQueen, Seann Walsh, Dane Baptiste, Janine Harouni, Larry Dean and Tom Parry are reconstructed as sketches

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: A girl drops a full spaghetti bolognaise over her iPad, a dashcam captures a tree almost crushing a car and a selfie is spoiled by a naked photo-bomber's bum

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking three contestants to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000

programme.title: Spy Kids 4: All the Time in the World (2011)
programme.desc: A secret agent decides to retire after having a baby, while her two stepchildren remain completely unaware of her exciting past. They stumble on to the truth when she is recalled to duty and, with the aid of two former junior spies, they help battle an evil mastermind who has the power to control time. Family adventure, starring Jessica Alba, Jeremy Piven, Rowan Blanchard and Mason Cook

programme.title: Turbo (2013)
programme.desc: A garden snail obsessed with motor racing gets sucked into a car's exhaust pipe, and emerges having gained super-speed. This stroke of luck allows him to realise his dream of competing against human drivers in the prestigious Indianapolis 500 race. Animated adventure, with the voices of Ryan Reynolds, Paul Giamatti and Samuel L Jackson

programme.title: Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom (2018)
programme.desc: Three years after the destruction of Jurassic World, Owen Grady and Claire Dearing are asked to return to the theme park to save its resident dinosaurs from the island's active volcano. During their mission, they uncover a conspiracy that may affect the entire planet. Action sequel, starring Chris Pratt, Bryce Dallas Howard, Rafe Spall and Toby Jones

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg gets a place at Brown University, but she is suspended for her application being fraudulent and has to prove she deserves to be there. Meanwhile, Brian tries to get fit

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: After Stan is injured, Francine nurses him back to health and he becomes dependent on her. The family discovers Tuttle is a reality TV star in Korea

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie auditions in drag for his favourite TV show after discovering it is only searching for girls, and soon becomes a hit with viewers. Meg shocks the entire city by dating a conventionally attractive boy

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian starts dating an older woman, but she has an accident and breaks her hip, forcing him to become her unofficial errand boy - and prompting the couple to re-evaluate their relationship

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie befriends a teenage pop star, but discovers a surprising secret about her. Meanwhile, Chris tries to convince his family that the evil monkey in his wardrobe is real. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Seth MacFarlane, Mila Kunis and Seth Green

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Francine becomes bored with her love life so tries to suggest different ways Stan can spice things up between them - and soon develops a passion for spanking. Meanwhile, Hayley tries to move on from Jeff being sent into outer space and agrees to go out on a dinner date with Snot

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna gets into an argument with Arianna at a silent auction, so Cleveland defends his wife's honour by throwing eggs at her adversary's house. He decides to do the same to all the people who have offended his family, but problems arise when he is accused of a murder he did not commit. Guest starring Arianna Huffington

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland begins to socialise with Dr Fist, but a well-meaning attempt to get in touch with the medic's old friends reveals a potentially life-threatening secret. Meanwhile, Junior and Rallo open a mobile catering business at school, but their enterprise faces competition

programme.title: Inception (2010)
programme.desc: A talented thief uses hi-tech devices to enter other people's dreams so he can steal their secrets. An industrialist hires him to perform a far more challenging job - to implant an idea into a corporate heir's mind, so he will think it is his own. However, the mission is compromised by the thief's own troubled psyche. Christopher Nolan's sci-fi thriller, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Elliot Page, Joseph Gordon-Levitt and Tom Hardy

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Inception (2010)
programme.desc: A talented thief uses hi-tech devices to enter other people's dreams so he can steal their secrets. programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Mim sustains an injury, leaving Roger with the responsibility of organising the team teas, and Alex's insecure girlfriend puts in an appearance. Cricketing comedy drama, with Brenda Blethyn and Robert Daws

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Nick takes a risk by returning home to look for Sam. Emma meets a handsome stranger. Tyrone feels the strain of supporting two households

programme.title: Charade (1963)
programme.desc: A distraught widow learns her husband was murdered for his part in a $250,000 robbery. The haul has gone missing, and his former accomplices are soon on her tail in the hunt for the missing proceeds. Not knowing where to turn, she takes up the offer of help from a dashing stranger, but is uncertain whether she can trust him. Romantic thriller, with Audrey Hepburn, Cary Grant, Walter Matthau and James Coburn

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls asleep during a flight from Paris to London, while a female passenger is murdered by a poisoned dart, leaving few clues to the killer's identity. Poirot soon discovers she was a money-lender, which may provide a motive for the killing. The investigation is turned on its head, however, when the victim's illegitimate daughter comes forward to claim the inheritance. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with David Firth, Jenny Downham and John Bleasdale

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's former CID colleague Jack Colby left the force under a cloud following a sex scandal involving villagers and officers at Causton police station. Now retrained as a probation officer, Jack is working at a halfway house for reformed criminals, so when he is bludgeoned to death the detective sets out to discover why the past would not leave him alone. With Chris Barrie, Edward Petherbridge, Sue Jenkins, Nigel Harrison and Christopher Fairbank

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby becomes an unwelcome guest at baron Ned Fitzroy's wedding to Beth Porteous when the maid of honour is found pinned to the wall with a dagger. Meanwhile, Cully gets cold feet about tying the knot with Simon and is disappointed to learn the honeymoon to New Zealand will have to be cut short. With his own daughter's nuptials to attend, Barnaby races to unearth the family secrets he is sure lie behind the Fitzroy tragedy. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Laura Howard

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: The arrival of Miss Lambe's forbidden love causes Charlotte to question her opinion of Sidney. Clara witnesses a moment between Esther and Sir Edward that raises her suspicions about their relationship, with devastating consequences for Esther. Period drama, starring Rose Williams and Theo James

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida is dismayed when handyman Jim Barnes is given his marching orders and devises a plan to get his job back

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger's pre-match strategy has a dramatic impact in the team's hotel room on the night before the Cromer game. Comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Aaron tries to track Liv down. Sam and Samson are shaken. Lydia is heartbroken

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When the sleuth's dentist is found dead just minutes after he leaves his surgery, Chief Inspector Japp believes it to be suicide - but Poirot has reason to think otherwise and embarks on his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is asked to investigate the theft of a car belonging to a friend of Mullett, but misjudges the grizzled Superintendent's capacity for tact and diplomacy and it is not long before he is taken off the case. Things take a shocking turn when the car finally turns up - with the body of a drug dealer in the boot. Detective drama, starring David Jason, with Melanie Thaw and Bruce Alexander

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper detective accepts an invitation to spend yuletide at the house of an elderly millionaire who seems intent on provoking his money-grubbing sons into murdering him. At first he tries hard to ignore the strained atmosphere and relax - but soon finds himself with a gruesome mystery on his hands. Agatha Christie whodunit, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When a spate of petty thefts culminates in a series of murders at a student hostel, Miss Lemon persuades the Belgian sleuth to investigate. As ever, he receives support from Chief Inspector Japp. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis and Paris Jefferson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a victim of fraud is found murdered on a golf course - but his inquiries are complicated by the discovery of a tramp's mutilated body nearby. To make matters worse, beautiful singer Bella Duveen is also around, seemingly intent on helping herself to a grim souvenir. David Suchet stars, with Hugh Fraser, Bill Moody and Jacinta Mulcahy

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The successful businesswoman behind an empire of beauty salons throws a lavish party on board a boat on the River Tyne - only for her sister to be found dead in the water. Vera's investigation uncovers the possibility that the victim may have been in an abusive relationship - but may also have been about to bring a terrible secret to light that threatened her entire family

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. She suspects the bones belong to a notorious criminal who was once a friend of her father and has been missing for more than 20 years. When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. Brenda Blethyn stars in an adaptation of Ann Cleeves' 2017 Vera Stanhope novel The Seagull

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Nick takes a risk by returning home to look for Sam. Emma meets a handsome stranger. Tyrone feels the strain of supporting two households

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Aaron tries to track Liv down. Sam and Samson are shaken. Lydia is heartbroken

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: A crumpled note, which Ida finds in the pocket of Robert's jacket, provokes a family crisis when everyone jumps to the same conclusion, that he's having an affair with a woman named Polly

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne is worried that Darren is plotting revenge against her and Nick. Vera interviews new barmaids for the Rovers. Audrey warns Spider that she can fight a dirty election

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Spider's attempts to raise election funds fall flat. Steve urges Jim to accept his help. Jack and Alec are aghast when Vera employs a young, hunky barman

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ollie bunks off school and follows Zak around Hotten. Chris tells Zoe she's wasting her time with Frankie. Bernice tries to give Tricia elocution lessons

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Viv attempts to apologise to Kelly. Ollie bunks off school again and ends up in danger. Kelly is not impressed with Tricia's efforts to be a lady

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A man begins receiving nuisance calls after a mysterious stranger moves into the village. Meanwhile, Gina's love life hots up in the kitchen - with serious repercussions. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida finds her new home uncomfortable, until a special delivery arrives

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The rent money disappears, forcing Jo to consider selling a prized possession. Flat-share comedy, with Sally Thomsett, Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Sam and Foyle are shot at and almost run over by a speeding vehicle, and discover the incident is part of an illicit fuel racket. An undercover investigation reveals the detective's son Andrew to be involved, and the discovery that a driver at the petrol company has been murdered complicates matters further. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jo makes the rash assumption that a man has taken violent steps to deal with his truanting son, and Greengrass sets up an illegal stud with a horse-training pal. Meanwhile, PC Ventress starts behaving oddly. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Angie and Sean wait in the hospital for news on Ollie. Tricia is bitter about Bernice's good luck. Sean and Zak scuffle with each other over what happened to Ollie

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice is nervous about the handover of the pub. Graham lets himself into Kathy's cottage to tidy it up for her return. Tricia takes a shine to Richie

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alec and Jack want rid of Sandy, but Vera is adamant he stays. Roy sets off to Amsterdam to find Hayley. Greg is alarmed when Maxine suggests he moves in with her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim decides to give up alcohol. Emily buys Spider a suit to help with his campaign. Hayley is happy to see Roy, but neither of them know where they stand

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth heads to Lake Windermere for a holiday with faithful assistant Hastings, only to find his crime-solving skills are once again needed to investigate a murder. The only witness to the crime is a dog, leaving Poirot with a difficult task to find the killer. David Suchet stars, with Kate Buffery, Patrick Ryecart and Julia St John

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A body is discovered on the Northumberland moors, and identified as a woman who made a distressed 999 call on the night of her death - suggesting she may have been abducted by someone she knew. Vera takes on the case, and discovers the victim was a controversial figure in her home town, who had a complicated relationship with her family and a number of well-hidden secrets

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle investigates what he believes to be a burglary at the headquarters of a multinational company, and the murder of a Home Guard member during a large-scale exercise on the Sussex Downs soon leads him to believe the firm are conducting covert business with the Nazis - and that his former police sergeant may be involved. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell

programme.title: Rising Damp (1980)
programme.desc: Seedy landlord Rigsby takes in a seemingly respectable new lodger who jeopardises his efforts to romance tenant Miss Jones - but the new arrival may not be all he appears. Meanwhile, the addition of a rugby playing art student to the house sparks a fitness craze that leads Rigsby to enter a boxing match. Comedy based on the 1970s sitcom, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington
